nodes.label: Database choice v1
nodes.content: Originally chose SQLite for local development only

nodes.label: Database choice v2
nodes.content: Latest decision: PostgreSQL for production and local parity

nodes.label: JWT refresh race
nodes.content: Refresh token rotation has a race condition in the auth middleware

nodes.label: dark mode preference
nodes.content: User strongly prefers dark mode on all interfaces

nodes.label: FastAPI backend choice
nodes.content: Chose FastAPI over Flask because async support is critical

nodes.label: Redis session caching
nodes.content: Redis is used for caching session tokens with TTL support

nodes.label: Neo4j concurrency note
nodes.content: SQLite struggles with concurrent writes, which pushed the team toward Neo4j

nodes.label: GraphQL rejected
nodes.content: Team decided against GraphQL due to lack of expertise and REST being sufficient

nodes.label: Rate limiting per tenant
nodes.content: API rate limiting is implemented per-tenant at 120 requests per minute globally

nodes.label: Kubernetes rollout strategy
nodes.content: Production uses RollingUpdate strategy with a 25% max surge

nodes.label: Embedding model choice
nodes.content: all-MiniLM-L6-v2 chosen for local semantic embeddings, no API key needed

nodes.label: Dedup threshold conservative
nodes.content: Deduplication threshold kept at 0.97 until larger fixture set validates lowering it

nodes.label: Token efficiency claim
nodes.content: Waggle retrieves context in approximately 4x fewer tokens than chunked-vector RAG baselines

nodes.label: MCP stdio transport
nodes.content: Claude Desktop and Codex connect via stdio MCP transport

nodes.label: Multi-tenant isolation
nodes.content: Each tenant has a separate node namespace; cross-tenant queries are blocked

nodes.label: Backup export format
nodes.content: Graph backups are exported as portable JSON files via export_graph_backup

nodes.label: Conflict detection edge
nodes.content: When a new node contradicts an existing node, a contradicts edge is added automatically

queries.name: database_recent_decision
queries.query: what did we decide recently about the database

queries.name: auth_token_duration
queries.query: how long do auth tokens last

queries.name: theme_preference_paraphrase
queries.query: which interface theme does the user want

queries.name: framework_choice
queries.query: which backend framework did we settle on

queries.name: sqlite_reason
queries.query: why did we stop using sqlite

queries.name: auth_bug_adversarial
queries.query: there's a bug in authentication around token refresh
queries.note: Neither 'race condition' nor 'rotation' — tests semantic generalization on auth domain

queries.name: api_throttle_adversarial
queries.query: what is our policy for throttling clients
queries.note: Paraphrase: throttle != rate limit, clients != tenant

queries.name: graphql_history
queries.query: did we ever consider switching away from REST
queries.note: Query is about REST history; only connects to the GraphQL rejection node

queries.name: vector_search_implementation
queries.query: how does semantic search work without an API key
queries.note: No overlap with node label — tests deep semantic resolution

queries.name: deployment_surge
queries.query: what deployment strategy are we using in prod
queries.note: prod == production, strategy is present but surrounded by other K8s facts

queries.name: db_original_choice_temporal
queries.query: what was the original database before we changed it
queries.note: Requires temporal disambiguation — v1 not v2 — in presence of a newer decision node

queries.name: k8s_surge_indirect
queries.query: how much extra capacity do we spin up during a rollout
queries.note: Asks about 25% max surge concept without using the word 'surge' or 'strategy'

queries.name: token_cost_indirect
queries.query: how does waggle reduce context window usage
queries.note: No token/RAG terminology overlap — must infer from task domain

queries.name: client_connection_adversarial
queries.query: how does Claude Desktop connect to the memory server
queries.note: stdio/MCP not mentioned in query — tests technology-specific semantic recall

queries.name: tenant_data_separation
queries.query: can one user's agent see another user's memory
queries.note: Framed as a privacy/security question; no overlap with 'namespace' or 'tenant'

queries.name: conflict_mechanism
queries.query: what happens when two stored facts contradict each other
queries.note: Asks about mechanism (what happens) not about the name (contradicts edge)

queries.name: backup_format_indirect
queries.query: can I migrate my memory to a different machine
queries.note: Migration framing; no mention of 'backup', 'JSON', or 'export'
